feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Process Automation
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Process Automation Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - Notification step fails with "this.certificates is null"
entry.content: Issue resolved, no longer needed

entry.title: Question on Automatic Clearing Rules – Do All Selected Criteria Need to Match?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a question regarding the Automatic Clearing of Open Items configuration in SAP (transaction OB74).

When defining clearing rules, SAP allows us to specify up to five matching criteria. My understanding is that all selected criteria must match in order for automatic clearing to occur.
Can you please confirm if this is correct?

Additionally, we have a business requirement where different combinations of fields should trigger clearing independently, for example:

  • Clear if Amount + Reference (XBLNR) match
  • Clear if Amount + Billing Document (VBLEN) match
  • Clear if Amount + Assignment (ZUONR) match

Is there a way to configure SAP so that each of these combinations works as an independent clearing rule, instead of requiring all fields in the OB74 configuration to match at the same time?

Any guidance on how to set this up—or whether multiple parallel clearing rules are possible—would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: SBPA Desktop Automation -- How to select a specific value from ARIBA DDM dropdown(bh=DDM) at runtime
entry.content:
Environment:

  • SAP Build Process Automation (Cloud Studio)
  • Desktop Agent, ARIBA framework application
  • Browser: Microsoft Edge
  • Target: SAP Ariba Log Settings page

Problem:

I need to programmatically select a specific value from an ARIBA DDM dropdown (bh=DDM, role=combobox) during a bot automation. The value to select changes dynamically per loop iteration (node name from a NodeItem loop variable).

The dropdown contains 342 items and uses virtual rendering -- items are only loaded into the DOM when the dropdown is open and filtered by typing.

What I have declared:

  • e_logicalNode -- the DDM element (DIV bh=DDM role=combobox)
  • e_allDDI -- collection of all DDI items (bh=DDI role=option, 342 elements)

What I have tried:

Attempt 1 -- Click DDM + Keystroke to filter + Click e_allDDI[0]:

  • Click e_logicalNode opens the dropdown
  • Keystroke with expression editor types the node name (confirmed resolving correctly via Log Message)
  • Click e_allDDI[0] always clicks the first item in the full unfiltered collection, not the filtered result

Attempt 2 -- Click DDM + Keystroke + {Return}:

  • Click e_logicalNode opens the dropdown
  • Keystroke {Ctrl}A + {Delete} to clear filter, then type node name, then {Return}
  • Dropdown opens but Keystroke does not interact with the filter -- default node remains selected
  • Root cause suspected: clicking the DDM div opens the popup but focus does NOT land on the filter input inside the popup

Attempt 3 -- Script Element (WEB framework):

  • Created a WEB framework application and used Script Element activity to execute JavaScript
  • All JavaScript approaches fail: document, window, and element are all undefined in the Script Element content context
  • Error: SCRIPTITEM failed in content context: Cannot read properties of undefined

Confirmed facts:

  • DDI items ARE in the DOM even when dropdown is closed (document.querySelector('[awname*=C1_UI1]') found via browser console)
  • mousedown + mouseup + click events on a DDI item via browser console DO select it correctly
  • The filter input inside the DDM popup is a separate element that requires explicit focus before typing

Question:

  1. How do I correctly select a specific value from an ARIBA DDM dropdown at runtime in SBPA Desktop Automation?
  2. Is there a native SBPA activity for ARIBA DDM value selection (similar to SAPUI5 Combo Box Set Value activity)?
  3. If Keystroke is the correct approach, how do I ensure focus lands on the filter input inside the DDM popup after clicking the DDM?

entry.title: Trigger SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from SAP Build Apps (Form & API Based)
entry.content:
Trigger SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from SAP Build Apps (Form & API Based)

When building applications using SAP Build Apps, a common requirement is to trigger workflows created in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA).

However, many developers face confusion:

Can we trigger a Form-based process directly from Build Apps?

👉 Short answer: No — Build Apps can only trigger workflows via API.
But don’t worry — there’s a proper workaround.

In this blog, we’ll cover:

  • ✅ Triggering API-based workflows
  • ✅ Triggering Form-based workflows via API workaround
  • ✅ End-to-end configuration

Understanding the Problem

In SAP Build Process Automation:

Trigger TypeCan Build Apps Trigger Directly?
Form TriggerNo
API TriggerYes

Reason:
Build Apps communicates via REST APIs, not UI forms.

ℹ️To Integrate Build Apps with Process Automation we need BTP Destination
Create Destination in SAP BTP with the same Name "sap_process_automation_service_user_access". Authentication can be either OAuth2ClientCredentials or OAuth2JWTBearer but add the correct Client ID and Client Secret ID from Service key from the instance of SAP Build Process Automation. Additional Properties are important to get the success integration. Finally save and check the connection.

Approach 1: API-Based Workflow Trigger 

Step 1: Create Process with API Trigger

  • Open your BPA project
  • Create a Process
  • Select Start Event → API Trigger

    Define Input Schema:
    {
    "FirstName": "string"
    }

Step 2: Deploy the Process

Click the three dots in SAP lobby of deployed process and publish to library. So that only you can integrate deployed process in SAP Build Apps.

After deployment, you will get:

  • Workflow API endpoint
  • Process Definition ID

Step 3: Configure SAP Build Apps

In SAP Build Apps:

1.Go to App Settings

Click Enable Authentication, Choose SAP BTP Authentication. Click Ok on the Popup.
If already SAP BTP Authentication added in App Setting Leave the Above Step

2.Integrate Workflow Process

Go to Integrations Tab Click Add Integration Button, Choose SAP Build Library. Under Processes you can select the published workflow process and  Enable process

3.Create UI

  1. Input fields:
    -> FirstName
  2. Start Process Button
    Add Logic to the above Button.

In Logic window, Add Trigger Process, choose the enabled Process and configure the input parameter as object with properties. To show status you can add either Alert/ Toast Message.
     

✅ Result

  • User give inputs in Build Apps
  • Clicks Submit
  • Workflow starts in BPA

Approach 2: Form-Based Process 

⚠️ Important Concept

Even if your process uses a Form Start Event,
👉 there is NO direct API to submit that form

So this will NOT work:
❌ Build Apps → Submit BPA Form directly

✅ Correct Workaround

🔄Convert Form Input → API Payload in SAP Build Apps

Step 1: Create Form Based Trigger Workflow in SAP Build Process Automation.

    • Here I took Leave Approval Application Workflow and deploy it. Trigger the process once then you can see the definition Id in Monitoring tab for this triggered workflow. Copy that ID for later use.
        

Step 2 : Create Build Apps Application 

  • Add SAP BTP Authentication in App Settings if not you not done yet.
  • Go to Integration tab and create a data entity by selecting SAP BTP Destination Rest API integration.
    • In Base, Choose the destination and add resource schema. ⚠️ Do not add common request header for token passing

    • Click on create and Enable it.
      • Set the relative path and query - Add static value "/public/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances".
      • Set Request Method as POST. 
      • Add Request Headers as List -> Key : Content-Type, Value :  application/json.
      • Add Request Body Mapper by wrap the data as json. and Save the data entity.
        ENCODE_JSON({"definitionId":"<value of the definitionId which is copied from Monitoring tab in SAP BPA>", "context": {<all parameters present in startEvent of input schema (you can refer in monitoring tab of the triggered process>}})


  • Create UI with required input fields. Add Button and configure the process flow in logic canvas.
    • Add create record and configure the resource which is create by following above steps. Set the records as object with properties. Add Toast Message/ Alert for your purpose.
    • Test the App, you will get the result and also you can cross check with monitoring tab in SAP BPA for the triggered process

⚠️ Common Issues

❌ Invalid Token 

  • Wrong OAuth configuration
  • No proper Addition Properties

❌ 401 Unauthorized

  • Client ID / Secret issue

❌ Workflow Not Starting

  • Wrong definitionID
  • Incorrect payload format

These can resolve by adding correct input configuration and Correct destination configuration.

🔄 Architecture Overview

SAP Build Apps (UI Form) ➡️ REST API Call (OAuth) ➡️ SAP Build Process Automation ➡️ Workflow Execution
 
🎯 Best Practices
✔ Validate payload before sending
✔ Use destinations for secure connectivity
✔ Always use inspect to check the console for error

Conclusion

🎉 And that’s a wrap!
You’ve now unlocked the power of triggering workflows in SAP Build Process Automation directly from SAP Build Apps using API-driven integration.
What started as a simple requirement—triggering a workflow—has evolved into a powerful architectural pattern:

  • 🎯 Build Apps handling the user experience
  • ⚙️ SAP BPA orchestrating the process logic
  • 🔗 APIs acting as the bridge between them

By moving beyond Form Triggers and embracing an API-first approach, you’re not just solving a limitation—you’re designing a solution that is scalable, flexible, and enterprise-ready.

💡 Imagine the possibilities:
From employee requests and approvals to complex cross-system automations—this pattern can power countless real-world scenarios with efficiency and control.

🚀 And this is just the beginning.
You can take it further by integrating additional services, enhancing validations, or building intelligent, event-driven workflows across your SAP landscape.

If you found this helpful or have ideas to expand this approach, feel free to connect and share your thoughts!

Let’s build smarter automation together.

LinkedIn : https://www.linkedin.com/in/abirami-t/

entry.title: My Inbox Build Process Automation Stuck Loading
entry.content:
Hi from 16-04-2026

We are facing the issue that in Build Process Automation My Inbox is loading Correctly the Approval and form notification its stuck in spinning and not giving anything.

Attaching the Screen shot how to resolve it i checked some 403 forbidden error is coming. All my destination are fine and working what is an issue there can some one please check i have all the authorisation is there a specific destination that needs to be configured ??

SAP Build Process Automation  #My Inbox

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: My Inbox stuck in loading
entry.content:
Good day,

There seems to be an issue currently with the My Inbox in SAP Build/SAP Build Process Automation. We are receiving an error 403 when viewing the My Inbox page. Our destinations are properly defined, and have not made any changes since Friday. It still persists up to this day. 

Anyone also experiencing this and have applied a fix?

 

Thank you very much!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - My Inbox is stuck at loading
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently facing an issue with My Inbox page of SAP Build Process Automation. It is currently not loading the tasks that are triggered in BPA and is just stuck in loading. Is there any fix for this?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Joule Studio: Why is build_default required if standard plan exists?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently setting up Joule Studio in SAP BTP and reviewing the prerequisites.

According to the documentation, Joule Studio requires an entitlement for SAP Build Process Automation with the service plan “build_default”.

However, in my subaccount I already have the “standard” (standard-application) plan assigned, which provides full production capabilities for SAP Build Process Automation (workflows, RPA, monitoring, etc.).

From my understanding:

  • The standard plan offers full functionality for productive use

  • The build_default plan seems to provide a more limited or base-level feature set

This raises a concern, as introducing or switching to build_default could potentially limit existing capabilities.

My questions are:

  1. Is the build_default plan technically required for Joule Studio, even if the standard plan is already available?

  2. Is there a dependency or integration requirement that specifically relies on build_default?

  3. Can both plans coexist without impacting existing Build Process Automation scenarios?

  4. What is the recommended entitlement setup for using Joule Studio in a productive BTP environment?

Any clarification or best practices would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Send Email Activity in SAP iRPA giving error
entry.content:
I was sending email via send email activity, it was working since last 6 months. Today it showed an error- SAP irpa core error - Failed to send email Outlook does not recognize one or more names. I can't understand what's wrong here.
Can someone please help me!!!!

entry.title: My Inbox Forward Option Not Working in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working on SAP Build Process Automation and facing an issue with the My Inbox Forward functionality.

Issue:
The "Forward" option in My Inbox is not working.

Details:

  • Application: SAP Build Process Automation (My Inbox)
  • Action: Forward Task to another user
  • Result: Action is not working / no response / failing

Expected Behavior:
The task should be forwarded successfully to another user.

What I Checked:

  • User roles and permissions
  • Workflow configuration
  • SAP documentation/comments

Still, the issue persists.

Questions:

  1. Is there any specific configuration required for Forward functionality?
  2. Are there any role/authorization restrictions?
  3. Is this a known issue or limitation?

Any help or guidance would be appreciated.

Thanks & Regards,
Umapathi

entry.title: BPA - Internal Server Error Issue
entry.content:
So I am using Build Process Automation under free tier and it is giving me an error very frequently after some time when I create a new project in BPA this Issue Comes All my Automation If I want to check give me Internal Server Error and its happening again and again. 
Does anyone know why is this and how can I solve it.

SAP Build #Error

entry.title: Deployment Error In SAP Build Process Automation // Deployment request timeout
entry.content:
Hi,

We are facing recurring errors when deploying SBPA projects. Everything was working fine until now, but since today it has become impossible to deploy our projects: we keep having a "Deployment request timeout" error.

 

It seems like the issues reported here or here coming from SAP's side is happening again...

Thanks for your feedback.

BR,

LM.

entry.title: Build Process Automation Capturing Element in Automation
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to capture the Green Tick in STMS_IMPORT that shows that the TR is Imported Successfully but i am not able to do that. Does anyone know how to use RPA to know that the TR is imported or not would love some advice cause that icon is not giving me anything nor code nothing how can i capture that icon.

SAP Build

entry.title: Error when adding event trigger to process: Unable to recognize the artifact type [ai-skill/agent]
entry.content:
When creating an event trigger for a process I get the following error: Unable to recognize the artifact type [ai-skill/agent]

If I close the dialog the trigger has been added but I subsequently get an error on deployment ("The schema reference "#/definitions/datetime"(#/definitions/datetime) cannot be resolved').

It seems to happen regardless of which process I use, and it doesn't matter which event I pick.  The same thing happens if I create my event project from a file.  I can deploy a project created previously with an event trigger.

This request is the cause: https://[route here]build.cloud.sap/api/internal/irpa/artifactory/v1/projects/[process id here]/artifacts?whereTypeIs=bpi.process%2Cai-skill%2Fagent&page=0&size=1000&orderBy=name+asc

If I remove the ",ai-skill/agent" then the request is fine and it returns header details for my process

The sub-account does not have any AI entitlements

Can anyone create event triggers right now?

Seems very similar to this recent post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/error-quot-unable-to-recognize-the-artifact-type-notificationtemplate-quot/qaq-p/14087373

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Cannot add rules in Decision (Trial)
entry.content:
I’m using SAP Build Process Automation on an SAP BTP Trial account.
I can create a Decision and define input parameters.
However, I cannot persist / save output parameters, and therefore I cannot add any rules.
In the Rules tab, clicking "+" does nothing and no decision table is created. 
Environment:
- SAP BTP Trial
- SAP Build Process Automation (free plan)
- Browsers tested: Chrome, Edge
- User has ProcessAutomation role collections 
Is this a known issue in Trial tenants?
Is there a workaround or fix?
Screenshots available.

entry.title: Desktop Agents in SAP BPA
entry.content: I want to purchase full commercial SAP Build Process Automation access immediately, including agent deployment and productive capabilities. Please provide the correct subscription path for an independent consultant based in UK?

entry.title: Exporting SAP Build Apps: Converting "Web and Mobile" to "Web Only" for Build-Default Plan
entry.content:
Hello,

I am moving my development work between tenants. My original project was created as a "Web and Mobile Application" in a tenant with a Free Plan. My new tenant is on the Build-Default plan.

When attempting to import my project, I am blocked by the issue described in KBA 3693320, as the Build-Default plan does not support "Web and Mobile" project types.

Since there is no "Convert to Web-Only" button in the UI, I am looking for the most efficient way to take my work with me.

I would like to know:

  1. Is there any way to export the project so that the target tenant accepts it as a "Web-Only" app?

  2. If direct import is impossible, is there a supported (or community-tested) way to copy objects, logic, or pages from the "Web and Mobile" project and paste them into a fresh "Web Only" project shell?

Thank you for your help!

entry.title: Exception handling for actions in SAP BPA
entry.content:
Hi,

A customer would like to be able to manage the response of an action even when it fails (e.g., with status codes 401, 404, etc.).

For example, we need to call an action to create a sales order, but the action may fail because the customer ID does not exist. In this case, we don’t want the entire process to fail. Instead, we would like to handle the error, for instance, by allowing the user to correct the customer ID through a form.

I’ve seen that this capability has been delivered:
https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800003832&range=FIRST-LAST#;INNO=81C4C671B92A1EDFA2F214564741EB46

Is there any documentation available on this feature? How does it work?

Thank you!

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Import file dialog not working in screen recording in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I am trying to capture the screens of SAP GUI tcode(UDM_DISPUTE) using recorder(automatic) in SAP Build Process Automation. My SAP Logon version is 800 32 bit. My RPA agent version is 3.44.104

There is one step where I need to upload attachment to the tcode like this. It is the same GOS(Generic Object Service) which handles the attachment. 

Generally, upon clicking the button, the import file dialog opens like below -

But, when I am capturing the same screen using screen recorder, upon clicking the create attachment the above windows popup dialog doesn't come anymore but the below popup comes -

 

But F4 value help for the directory and file name is not working. Besides providing the correct directory and file name also doesn't locate the file successfully. So, I am not able to proceed to continue capturing the screens further. 

Please suggest how should I proceed here.

Thanks,
Mainak

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Stable selection of dynamic dropdown items in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am building an automation to handle the "Anonymise Candidate Profiles" process in SAP SuccessFactors Admin Centre. I need to search for a candidate and select the correct one from a dropdown by matching the Email ID from an Excel sheet.

Current Problem: My current logic using "Keystroke Down" is unstable; it frequently skips items or fails to update the selection. Even when using a Collection (LI) with a For Each loop, the UI does not consistently register the click, and the automation sometimes retrieves technical metadata (JSON) instead of the visible text.

Technical Details:

  • Target Element: Li captured as a collection (Class: "fd list item").

  • Logic: Iterate through the collection -> Get text -> Compare with Excel Email -> Click matching index.

  • Challenge: Standard click/keystroke activities are not triggering the SuccessFactors selection event reliably.

Question: How can I achieve a stable selection in this specific UI? Is there a recommended Custom Script or a specific UI5/web event I should trigger to ensure the dropdown value is selected and the screen refreshes for the next candidate?

Attached are my recognition criteria and flow screenshots.

Any help is appreciated!

Thanks,

Narenthiran Annamalai.
